commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c7ba71855d6608a6a24a2a7cc04ee634bd6ee199)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an id-set guard around Tool Search catalog registration, tests direct same-session reuse/change behavior, and also changes the GitHub labeler workflow to tolerate size-label API failures.

PR surface: Source +23, Tests +62, Config +23. Total +108 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high-confidence: the linked issue includes repeated embedded gateway logs, and current source applies/logs Tool Search cataloging per embedded attempt while clearing that catalog during cleanup. I did not run a live Matrix/embedded turn in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed because the remaining work requires cache lifetime/equality design and separating an unrelated workflow change, not a safe mechanical repair on this branch.

target.title: fix(tool-search): skip catalog rebuild when tool set is unchanged
target.body:
## Summary

When Tool Search is enabled, `applyToolSearchCatalog` rebuilds and re-registers the full tool catalog on every conversation turn, even when the available tool set has not changed. This wastes CPU time on redundant `Map` operations and catalog entry construction each turn.

This PR adds a fast `catalogMatchesEntries` id-set comparison that short-circuits the rebuild when the incoming tool entries match the already-registered catalog. Both `applyToolSearchCatalog` (session-level) and `applyToolCatalogCompaction` (compaction-level) now skip `registerToolSearchCatalog` when the catalog is unchanged.

**Changes:**

- Add `catalogMatchesEntries(existing, incoming)` helper that checks id-set equality.
- Guard both `registerToolSearchCatalog` call sites with the match check.
- Add two tests: one verifying repeated calls are reference-stable no-ops, one verifying changed tool sets correctly trigger a rebuild.

Closes #86887

## Verification

- `pnpm test src/agents/tool-search.test.ts` — all 23 tests pass
- `pnpm format:check` — clean
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` — 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `pnpm build` — successful; fix confirmed in compiled bundle

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Tool Search catalog is redundantly rebuilt every conversation turn even when the tool set has not changed, causing unnecessary computation per turn (issue #86887).
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 15.5, Node v22.19, OpenClaw built from patched source on branch `fix/tool-search-catalog-reuse`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

  ```bash
  cd /Users/sebastientardif/openclaw
  git checkout fix/tool-search-catalog-reuse
  pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
  pnpm build
  grep -n "catalogMatchesEntries" dist/pi-tools-CSW74DDX.js
  ```

- **Evidence after fix:** terminal output from the patched build confirms the guard is active in the production bundle at all three sites:

  ```console
  $ grep -n "catalogMatchesEntries" dist/pi-tools-CSW74DDX.js
  353:function catalogMatchesEntries(existing, incoming) {
  534:if (!catalogMatchesEntries(params.catalogRef?.current ?? (key ? sessionCatalogs.get(key) : void 0), catalog)) registerToolSearchCatalog({
  786:if (!catalogMatchesEntries(params.catalogRef?.current ?? (key ? sessionCatalogs.get(key) : void 0), catalog)) registerToolSearchCatalog({
  ```

  Terminal confirms the catalog correctly reuses across repeated turns and rebuilds when the tool set changes:

  ```console
  ✓ skips catalog rebuild when the tool set is unchanged across turns 0ms
  ✓ rebuilds catalog when the tool set changes between turns 0ms
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The compiled bundle at `dist/pi-tools-CSW74DDX.js` wraps both `registerToolSearchCatalog` calls behind a `catalogMatchesEntries` guard. Terminal confirms repeated application with the same tool set is a reference-stable no-op (catalog object identity preserved), while a changed tool set correctly triggers a rebuild.
- **What was not tested:** Multi-agent sessions where different agents contribute different tool sets to the same session catalog concurrently.
target.author: sebtardif
